questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: In the energy pyramid shown, only the producer (trophic-1) energy is marked. Assuming 10% transfer per trophic level, the energy available at trophic level $4$ is _____ kcal/m²/yr.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each transfer retains ~10%: $E_n=E_0(0.1)^n$, with $E_0$ read from the pyramid.

**Working.**
$$10000\times0.1^{3}=10.$$

**Answer.** $10\,\text{kcal/m}^2/\text{yr}$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: An ecosystem has gross primary productivity $1000\,\text{g C/m}^2/\text{yr}$ and autotroph respiration $400\,\text{g C/m}^2/\text{yr}$. The net primary productivity is _____ g C/m²/yr.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\text{NPP}=\text{GPP}-R_{\text{auto}}$.

**Working.**
$$1000-400=600.$$

**Answer.** $600$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: An ecosystem has gross primary productivity $1500\,\text{g C/m}^2/\text{yr}$ and autotroph respiration $600\,\text{g C/m}^2/\text{yr}$. The net primary productivity is _____ g C/m²/yr.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\text{NPP}=\text{GPP}-R_{\text{auto}}$.

**Working.**
$$1500-600=900.$$

**Answer.** $900$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A population grows exponentially at intrinsic rate $r=0.02\,\text{yr}^{-1}$. Reading the initial size $N_0$ from the figure, after $10$ years it numbers _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $N_t=N_0e^{rt}$, with $N_0$ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$1000\,e^{0.02\times10}=1221.$$

**Answer.** $1221$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A population grows logistically at intrinsic rate $r=0.3\,\text{yr}^{-1}$. Reading the carrying capacity $K$ from the growth curve shown, the maximum rate of population increase ($rK/4$) is _____ individuals/yr.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Max $\dfrac{dN}{dt}=\dfrac{rK}{4}$ at $N=K/2$, with $K$ read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.3\times10000}{4}=750.$$

**Answer.** $750$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: The stable, self-perpetuating community reached at the end of ecological succession is the
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Succession culminates in a climax community in equilibrium with the environment.

**Working.**
Climax community.

**Answer.** Climax community

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: Which are recognised biodiversity hotspots in/around India?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Western Ghats, Himalaya and Indo-Burma are global hotspots; the Thar is not (low endemism/richness threshold).

**Working.**
First three.

**Answer.** Western Ghats, Himalaya, Indo-Burma

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A diesel-contaminated soil is cleaned in place by injecting air and nutrients to stimulate indigenous hydrocarbon-degrading bacteria, with no excavation. This technique is best classified as
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Treating soil where it lies by stimulating native microbes with O₂/nutrients is in-situ bioremediation (bioventing); ex-situ methods require excavation, and incineration/S-S are not biological.

**Working.**
No excavation + stimulate native microbes ⇒ in-situ.

**Answer.** In-situ bioremediation

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A hyperaccumulator crop yields $20\,\text{t/ha}$ of dry biomass containing $500\,\text{mg/kg}$ of nickel. The nickel phytoextracted per hectare in one harvest is _____ g/ha.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Metal extracted $=$ dry biomass $\times$ tissue concentration. With biomass $20\,\text{t/ha}=20{,}000\,\text{kg/ha}$ and $500\,\text{mg/kg}$: $(20{,}000)(500)/1000$ g.

**Working.**
$$20{,}000\times500/1000=10{,}000\,\text{g/ha}.$$

**Answer.** $10\,000\,\text{g/ha}$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: Anaerobic digestion of $1000\,\text{kg}$ of volatile solids yields biogas at $0.4\,\text{m}^3/\text{kg VS}$, of which $60\%$ is methane (calorific value $36\,\text{MJ/m}^3$). The recoverable energy is _____ MJ.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Energy $=$ VS $\times$ gas yield $\times$ CH₄ fraction $\times$ calorific value.

**Working.**
$$1000\times0.4\times0.6\times36=8640\,\text{MJ}.$$

**Answer.** $8640\,\text{MJ}$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A field device estimates wastewater BOD within minutes by measuring the respiration of immobilised microbes through a dissolved-oxygen electrode. The biological recognition element and the transducer are, respectively,
options: immobilised microorganisms and the dissolved-oxygen electrode
the DO electrode and the microbes
questions.solution:
**Concept.** In a microbial BOD biosensor the immobilised microbes are the bio-recognition element; the O₂ they consume is sensed by the DO electrode (the transducer).

**Working.**
Recognition = microbes; transducer = DO electrode.

**Answer.** Microbes; DO electrode
